title: Failure Doctrine Lab
public_claim: The Failure Doctrine Lab is a governed MuranoOS-native room for transforming a ~500-item raw corpus of intelligence collection-and-analysis failure statements into a disciplined doctrine workspace: an append-only environment of projects — raw corpus items — mechanisms — evidence — controls/indicators — exports.
distinctive_mechanism: It converts an archive or handoff into three inspectable decisions: what the work product is for, which component pieces carry the work, and what action would turn it into a live room, paper, method, or software instrument.
use_value: It preserves the distinctive design logic for Failure Doctrine Lab, so the portfolio can show the actual mechanism and status instead of reducing the project to a file-count or handoff label.
